channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 Chinese investment in Europe surges to 7-year high despite trade tensions
item.description: Chinese FDI in Europe hit a seven-year peak, but analysts warn the pipeline of new projects is drying up as investors face pressure from both Beijing and European capitals.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Over 160 oil tankers stranded in Gulf as Iran tightens Hormuz grip
item.description: More than 160 tankers carrying approximately 160 million barrels of oil remain stuck as Iran consolidates control over the Strait of Hormuz, creating one of the worst shipping bottlenecks in decades.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦 Russia arming missile warheads with depleted uranium, Ukrainian intelligence says
item.description: Ukraines SBU security service found depleted uranium projectiles in R-60 missile warheads, raising concerns about Russias battlefield tactics and potential long-term contamination.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇱🇹⚠️ Lithuanian lawmakers shelter as drone incursion halts Vilnius air traffic
item.description: A drone violated Lithuanian airspace near the Belarusian border, triggering the first air raid alarm in the countrys history and forcing officials to shelter as NATO eastern flank tensions escalate.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran threatens to expand war beyond Middle East if US launches new attacks
item.description: The IRGC warned that any new US or Israeli strikes on Tehran would trigger retaliation in places we cannot even imagine, as diplomatic efforts to finalize a ceasefire deal continue.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: 🇮🇷🚢 Iran consolidates Strait of Hormuz control with island checkpoints and passage fees
item.description: A Reuters investigation reveals Iran has established a multi-tiered system for clearing vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, including government-to-government arrangements and transit fees, as nations seek to replenish dwindling energy supplies throttled by the war.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Rubio heads to NATO meeting amid European concerns over US reliability
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will attend NATO foreign ministers meeting in Sweden this week, where US plans to reduce troop levels in Europe and Trump policy inconsistency have raised anxiety about American commitment to the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Washington Post

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China and US reach limited trade agreements on Boeing jets and beef
item.description: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing aircraft and resume imports of some US beef products, marking one of the clearest signs yet of easing trade tensions between the two countries following Trump Beijing summit.
item.dc:creator: Wall Street Journal

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 EU reaches deal on US trade pact ahead of Trump tariff deadline
item.description: EU lawmakers and member states reached agreement to implement the trade pact with the United States, ending months of wrangling and likely averting higher US tariffs threatened by President Trump for July 4.
item.dc:creator: France24

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Confirms 200 Boeing Jet Purchase After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China announced it will buy 200 Boeing aircraft and seek an extension of the US-China tariff truce through November, signalling a stabilization in relations between the world's two largest economies following last week's Beijing summit.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 EU Strikes Deal to Finalize US Trade Agreement, Avert Tariffs
item.description: The European Union reached provisional agreement on legislation to remove import duties on US goods, a key part of a trade deal with Washington that would prevent higher US tariffs on EU products.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Approval Drops to 35% as Republican Support Softens
item.description: A new Reuters/Ipsos poll shows President Donald Trump's approval rating falling to 35%, with softening support among Republicans as the administration navigates the Iran crisis and domestic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Reinforces Northern Border Over Russian Offensive Plans
item.description: Ukraine will send reinforcements to its northern regions and step up diplomatic pressure on Belarus, citing intelligence that Russia plans a new offensive toward Kyiv from the north.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 💰 Fed Minutes Reveal Deep Divisions Over Rate Policy
item.description: The Federal Reserve's latest meeting minutes reveal significant disagreement among policymakers on interest rate direction and inflation severity, exposing the most divided Fed in a generation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Summons Israeli Envoy Over Ben Gvir Flotilla Footage
item.description: France summoned Israels ambassador after National Security Minister Ben Gvir posted a video taunting bound Gaza flotilla activists. Ireland's foreign minister also condemned the footage as appalling.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🇮🇱🇹🇷 Israeli Minister Calls Turkey an Enemy State
item.description: Israels culture minister said Turkey should be treated as an enemy state, warning it could become the next Iran or Qatar. The comments further strain already tense Israel-Turkey relations.
item.dc:creator: Middle East Eye

item.title: 🇪🇺🇱🇹 Baltic Lawmakers Press EU for Firm Support Against Russian Strike Threats
item.description: Lithuanian and Baltic lawmakers are urging the EU to provide firm backing as Moscow threatens strikes in response to comments about attacking the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇬🇧🛢️ UK Eases Russian Oil Sanctions as Fuel Prices Soar
item.description: Britain has waived sanctions on diesel and jet fuel processed from Russian crude in third countries, citing energy supply security as domestic fuel prices continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Oil Price

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ IDF Brigade Commander Seriously Wounded in Lebanon Drone Strike
item.description: The commander of the IDF's 401st Armored Brigade was seriously wounded when an explosive-laden drone struck his position in south Lebanon, amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🇱🇹 Lithuania evacuates leaders after drone alert near Vilnius airport
item.description: Lithuanian president and prime minister were moved to safety after a suspected drone was detected near Vilnius airport, highlighting security concerns on NATO eastern flank amid tensions with Russia and Belarus.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇨🇺 Rubio addresses Cuban people in video offering new US relationship
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio directly addressed Cubans in a video message, offering $100 million in aid and blaming former leader Raúl Castro for the country's shortages of electricity, food and fuel, while urging a new chapter in US-Cuba relations.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump says Iran negotiations in final stages amid Hormuz tensions
item.description: President Trump announced that negotiations with Iran are in their final stages while warning of further attacks if Tehran fails to reach a deal, as tankers exit the Strait of Hormuz and oil markets watch closely.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇸🇪 Rubio to visit Sweden for NATO meeting on burden sharing then India
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will attend a NATO foreign ministers meeting in Sweden to discuss greater burden sharing by allies, before traveling to India as the Trump administration reviews its global military commitments.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 Rubio Offers Cuba New Relationship with $100 Million in Aid
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio offered to forge a new relationship with Cuba in a video message, proposing $100 million in aid and blaming Cuban leaders for shortages of electricity, food and fuel.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧📉 UK Inflation Cools More Than Expected in April
item.description: British inflation fell more than expected in April, but the slowdown masks a tough outlook for households as Iran war-related costs are set to hit consumers harder later this year.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
